title: podman 4.6 on Ubuntu 22.04 - missing podmansh and overlayfs error
body:
### Issue Description

Installing podman 4.6 on Ubuntu 22.04 via the Alvistack project (https://software.opensuse.org/download/package?package=podman&project=home%3Aalvistack)

podmansh is not setup, this can be fixed via the ln -s command
when using an example quadlet, setting podmansh as a login shell results in this error and an immediate exit from /bin/sh

# cat > /etc/containers/systemd/users/${USER_ID}/podmansh.container << _EOF
[Unit]
Description=The Podmansh container
After=local-fs.target

[Container]
Image=registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora
ContainerName=podmansh
RemapUsers=keep-id
RunInit=yes
DropCapability=all
NoNewPrivileges=true

Exec=sleep infinity

[Install]
RequiredBy=default.target
_EOF

overlayfs: fs on '/home/podtest/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/compat638544074/lower1' does not support file handles, falling back to xino=off.




### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. install Ubuntu server 22.04
2. upgrade to Podman 4.6 via https://software.opensuse.org/download/package?package=podman&project=home%3Aalvistack
3. ln -s podman podmansh
4. use example quadlet with a new user, set the shell to podmansh

logon as that user, immediate exit after MOTD appears.


### Describe the results you received

User unable to logon to container.

### Describe the results you expected

Expected container to be fetched and user logged on.

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
@nigwil thanks for the report and for your interest in podmansh. The podman team doesn't maintain the alvistack repo you mentioned, so there's nothing we can do here. Your best option would be to contact them directly.

Closing..
